topics.title: Spring Boot Actuator & Observability
topics.description: Spring Boot Actuator exposes production-ready endpoints for monitoring and managing a running Spring Boot application. Key endpoints: /actuator/health (liveness/readiness for Kubernetes), /actuator/metrics (Micrometer metrics), /actuator/info (application metadata), /actuator/env (environment properties), /actuator/loggers (runtime log level changes), /actuator/httptrace (recent HTTP requests), /actuator/beans (all Spring beans), /actuator/threaddump (JVM thread dump). Micrometer is the metrics facade — it publishes to Prometheus, Datadog, CloudWatch, etc. without changing application code.
topics.story: Imagine your Spring Boot app is an aircraft in flight. Actuator is the cockpit instrument panel. The /health endpoint is the altitude indicator — shows if you're still airborne (UP/DOWN). /metrics is the fuel gauge and engine RPM — CPU, memory, request latency, DB pool usage. /loggers lets you flip switches mid-flight — turn DEBUG on for one package without restarting. /threaddump is the black box — shows all threads and what they're doing. In production, you expose only what the ops team needs, not the full cockpit to passengers (/actuator/env contains secrets!).
topics.analogy: Actuator = car's OBD-II port — mechanics plug in a diagnostic tool and get every sensor reading. /health = dashboard warning light (all green vs specific component red). /metrics = Micrometer = the OBD-II protocol standard — the same sensor data (tire pressure, engine temp) goes to different dashboards (Prometheus/Grafana, Datadog) without rewiring. @Timed = a stopwatch attached to a specific method that reports to every connected dashboard. Custom health indicator = adding a custom sensor (check if external payment API is reachable).
topics.memoryAnchor: Dependency: spring-boot-starter-actuator. Expose endpoints: management.endpoints.web.exposure.include=health,info,metrics,loggers. Security: secure /actuator/** with ROLE_ADMIN in SecurityConfig. Health groups: management.endpoint.health.group.readiness.include=db,redis. Micrometer: Counter (increments), Gauge (current value), Timer (latency + throughput), DistributionSummary (size distribution). @Timed('method.name') on a method. Custom health: implement HealthIndicator, return Health.up().withDetail('key','val').build(). Prometheus: add micrometer-registry-prometheus, scrape /actuator/prometheus. Info endpoint: management.info.env.enabled=true, info.app.version=@project.version@.
topics.firstPrinciples: Actuator endpoints are regular Spring MVC endpoints mapped under /actuator. Each endpoint is a Spring bean annotated with @Endpoint. The exposure is controlled separately from enablement: management.endpoint.health.enabled=true, management.endpoints.web.exposure.include=health. Micrometer uses a MeterRegistry — an in-memory store of all metrics. Registry implementations (PrometheusMeterRegistry, DatadogMeterRegistry) periodically push or expose metrics in the target system's format. Health checks are composited: if any component is DOWN, the aggregate status is DOWN. Custom HealthIndicators can check external dependencies.
topics.bruteForce: No observability: errors are discovered by users, not ops. Manual log parsing to find slow endpoints. JVM memory issues discovered only on OOM crash. No way to change log level without restarting — rolling restart loses in-flight requests. No health check for load balancer — traffic sent to unhealthy pods. Manual version tracking in spreadsheets.
topics.optimizedApproach: Actuator + Prometheus + Grafana: /actuator/prometheus scraped every 15s → time-series data in Prometheus → dashboards in Grafana. Kubernetes: liveness probe → /actuator/health/liveness, readiness probe → /actuator/health/readiness. Custom health indicators for DB, Redis, external APIs. @Timed on all service methods for p99 latency tracking. Structured logging with trace IDs (Micrometer Tracing). Alert rules in Prometheus: fire if error rate > 1% or p99 latency > 2s.
topics.whenToUse: Always include actuator in production Spring Boot apps. Expose /health always (Kubernetes probes need it). Expose /metrics, /loggers to internal monitoring network only. Restrict /env, /beans, /threaddump to ADMIN role — they expose sensitive data. Use management.server.port to run actuator on a separate port inaccessible to the internet.

examples.title: Actuator Configuration and Custom Health Indicator
examples.explanation: Custom HealthIndicators are the key pattern for production readiness: if the payment gateway is down, the pod's health is DOWN and Kubernetes stops routing traffic to it. The separate management port (8081) means actuator is never exposed through the public-facing load balancer — only internal monitoring tools reach it. Micrometer's Timer with publishPercentiles gives p99 latency — the critical SLA metric for backend APIs. The three-line change to log level via /actuator/loggers/com.myapp without restarting is extremely valuable during live incident investigation.
examples.realWorldUse: Kubernetes: liveness probe restart the container if app is deadlocked. Readiness probe removes the pod from service endpoints if DB is unreachable — no traffic to a pod that can't serve it. Prometheus scrapes /actuator/prometheus every 15s, stores time-series. Grafana dashboards query Prometheus: HTTP request rate, p99 latency, JVM heap usage, DB connection pool active/idle. PagerDuty alerts fire when error rate > 2% sustained for 5 minutes.

examples.title: @Timed, Custom Metrics, and Runtime Log Level Change
examples.explanation: Tags are the key to useful metrics: orders.created{status='success'} and orders.created{status='error'} are two separate time-series — you can graph success rate = success/(success+error). The Gauge for activeOrderCount is a live view — useful to detect if orders are stuck in processing. The runtime log level change is one of the highest-value Actuator features: during an incident, enable DEBUG for the affected service package, watch the logs, then reset without any deployment.
examples.realWorldUse: Spring Boot auto-registers Micrometer metrics for: HikariCP pool (active/idle/max connections), Tomcat thread pool (busy/max), JVM memory/GC, HTTP request rate/latency by endpoint. These appear in /actuator/prometheus automatically without any code. Prometheus alert: alert when hikaricp_connections_active / hikaricp_connections_max > 0.8 (pool 80% full) — warns before connection pool exhaustion causes timeouts.

problems.title: Configure Actuator for Production
problems.hint1: management.endpoints.web.exposure.include= lists endpoints. Default exposes only health.
problems.hint2: management.server.port=8081 runs actuator on a different port — essential for security.
problems.testCases:
Expose: health,info,metrics,prometheus,loggers
show-details: when-authorized
management.server.port=8081
info.app.version=@project.version@
liveness: livenessState, readiness: readinessState+db+redis

problems.title: Write a Custom HealthIndicator
problems.description:
Use Actuator's /loggers endpoint to change log levels at runtime without restart.

```bash
# View current levels:
GET /actuator/loggers/com.example.service
# Response: {"configuredLevel":"INFO","effectiveLevel":"INFO"}

# Temporarily enable DEBUG (no restart needed!):
POST /actuator/loggers/com.example.service
Content-Type: application/json
{"configuredLevel": "DEBUG"}

# Revert to INFO:
POST /actuator/loggers/com.example.service
{"configuredLevel": "INFO"}
```

**Security:** Secure `/actuator/**` — only allow admin role or restrict to internal network:
```java
.requestMatchers("/actuator/**").hasRole("ACTUATOR_ADMIN")
```

**Key insight:** Dynamic log level change is invaluable for production debugging without restart. Always secure actuator endpoints in production.
problems.hint1: Implement HealthIndicator interface, override health() method.
problems.hint2: Use System.currentTimeMillis() before and after the call to measure response time.
problems.testCases:
Health.up() with responseTimeMs and httpStatus=200
Health.down() with error and message on exception
Component name 'externalApi' appears in /actuator/health
Aggregate DOWN when any component is DOWN
problems.sampleOutput: 204 No Content; DEBUG logs now flowing for that package
